_instructions: The psychographic portrait of who all content is written for. Skills read this to calibrate tone, hooks, examples, and CTAs. Fill in with real specificity — not 'marketing manager' but 'B2B SaaS founder, 1–10 person team, posting on LinkedIn but getting no traction'.

primary.label: [SHORT LABEL — e.g. 'The Stuck Founder' or 'The Scaling Agency Owner']
primary.company_size: [e.g. 'solo to 10-person team' OR 'series A SaaS' OR 'established SME']
primary.what_they_tried: [WHAT SOLUTIONS THEY'VE ALREADY ATTEMPTED THAT DIDN'T WORK]
primary.embarrassing_gap: [WHAT THEY'RE EMBARRASSED TO ADMIT THEY DON'T KNOW]
primary.currently_believes: [THEIR CURRENT BELIEF ABOUT YOUR TOPIC AREA]
primary.misconception_to_correct: [THE WRONG BELIEF YOU WANT TO CHALLENGE]
primary.knows_but_doesnt_act_on: [WHAT THEY ALREADY KNOW IS TRUE BUT STRUGGLE TO EXECUTE]
primary.content_engages_with: [WHAT KIND OF CONTENT THEY ACTUALLY ENGAGE WITH]
language_they_use: [EXACT PHRASE THEY USE TO DESCRIBE THEIR PROBLEM 1]
language_they_hate: [PHRASE THEY CRINGE AT HEARING FROM PEOPLE IN YOUR SPACE 1]
primary.internal_monologue: [THEIR PRIVATE INTERNAL MONOLOGUE WHEN FACING THE PROBLEM YOU ADDRESS]
primary.trust_objection: [WHAT OBJECTION THEY HAVE BEFORE TRUSTING YOUR CONTENT]
primary.loyalty_trigger: [WHAT MAKES THEM A LOYAL FOLLOWER VS ONE-TIME READER]

secondary._instructions: Optional. Fill in if you have a meaningful secondary audience segment with different needs.
secondary.label: [LABEL]
